commit.message:
feat: use postly/v1 export format for git storage

Git commits now write the entire collection as a .postly.json file
(the same format as File → Export), so it can be imported by anyone
without git access via File → Import.

- export-import.ts: export buildExport, importData, tryParse,
  ExportRequest, ExportGroup, ExportCollection so they can be reused
- postly:git:commit: serializes the full collection via buildExport and
  writes <collection-slug>.postly.json to the repo root.
  Clears is_dirty on ALL requests in the collection after a successful push.
- discoverAndImport: scans for *.postly.json at repo root first;
  imports all groups/requests from the Postly format.
  Falls back to OpenAPI spec scanning if no .postly.json files found.
- preload/GitSourceView: remove the now-unused 'content' parameter
  from git.commit calls

commit.message:
fix: create collection before cloneOrPull so import never loses the collection

The previous fix moved collection creation before the scan loop, but
cloneOrPull was still the first line in discoverAndImport. If the clone
failed for any reason (credentials, network, git not in PATH), the
collection INSERT was never reached.

Fix:
- Create/find the collection as the very first step, before any network
  calls, so it's always persisted to disk regardless of clone outcome
- IPC handler now recovers gracefully: if discoverAndImport throws after
  the collection was written, we look it up and return it anyway so the
  UI can still navigate to the (empty) collection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: configurable colour palette via `flyline set-color` (#385)

Users had no way to configure flyline's UI colours. This adds a
`set-color` subcommand backed by the
[`parse-style`](https://crates.io/crates/parse-style) crate for
rich-syntax style strings.

## Changes

- **`settings.rs`** — `ColorPalette` struct holding `inline_suggestion`
and `matching_char` styles, with `dark()` (original) and `light()`
presets. Added as `color_palette` field on `Settings`.
- **`palette.rs`** — `static ACTIVE_PALETTE:
LazyLock<RwLock<ColorPalette>>` global; `set_active_palette()` updates
it. `matched_character()` and `history_inline_meta()` now read from the
global — no call-site changes required.
- **`lib.rs`** — `SetColor` subcommand + `ColorDefault` enum.
`parse_style()` manually converts `parse_style::Style` →
`ratatui::style::Style` (the project's custom ratatui fork uses
`ratatui-core 0.1.0-beta.0`, incompatible with `parse-style`'s crates.io
`ratatui-core 0.1.0` feature). Invalid style strings print an error and
return a usage exit code.
- **`README.md`** — help block updated; new "Colour palette" section
with syntax and examples.

## Usage

```bash
flyline set-color --default dark           # original palette (default)
flyline set-color --default light          # preset for light terminals
flyline set-color --inline-suggestion "dim italic"
flyline set-color --matching-char "bold green"
flyline set-color --matching-char "#ff8700"        # RGB hex
flyline set-color --default light --matching-char "bold blue"
```

Style strings follow [rich
syntax](https://rich.readthedocs.io/en/stable/style.html):
space-separated attributes (`bold`, `dim`, `italic`, `underline`,
`reverse`, `strike`, …) and colours by name, `color(N)`, `#rrggbb`, or
`rgb(r,g,b)`.

commit.message:
Allow FlowerRecipe to pass extra_env with test (#4322)

### Description
The Flower integration initializes an env dict inside `FlowerRecipe` but
currently ignores the `extra_env` parameter. This makes it impossible to
inject custom environment variables into the Flower job. This PR changes
`FlowerRecipe` to start from `extra_env` (if provided) and then set
`CLIENT_API_TYPE_KEY` on top. This keeps the required external client
API setting while allowing users to pass additional environment
variables through `extra_env`. Added
`test_flower_recipe_merges_extra_env` to
`tests/unit_test/recipe/flower_recipe_test.py` to verify that
`extra_env` entries are preserved and `CLIENT_API_TYPE_KEY` is set.

fix: deduplicate device IDs in MQTT topics

The connector ID, device ID, and device name were all set to the
hostname, producing topics like home/host_host_host/func/state.

Changes:

  all ID segments use consistent formatting

  duplicate a segment already present
- Add tests for deduplication behavior

For a device with hostname 'mFi-mPower', topics now look like

commit.message:
fix: register device with connector before adding functions

Functions cached their base topic and unique_id using the parent
device's full ID, but the full ID was empty because the device had
not yet been registered with the connector. This caused all MQTT
topics to start with 'home//' and all unique_ids to be non-unique.

Move registerDevice() before the function registration loop so
m_full_id is populated when functions call getFullId().

commit.message:
Robot-rescues-robot: swarm autonomy minigames with creative problem solving

New manual override scenarios where robots help each other:
- TIPPED ROBOT: dispatch rescue bot. Approach slow (fast = both tip),
  scan terrain, extend arm, lift slowly (fast = swing past vertical).
  The same terrain that tipped one can tip the rescuer.
- WHEEL JAM: regolith packed in drivetrain. Position helper, CO₂ burst,
  pick debris, test rotate (force it = stripped gears). Spirit died this way.
- SOLAR PANEL STUCK: actuator stalled at partial deploy. Image hinge,
  clear dust, pulse incrementally (force deploy = burned H-bridge).

Each has trap commands: the obvious fast/force option always damages.
The correct path is slow, methodical, diagnostic-first.
Real Mars ops: creativity within constraints, patience over speed.

commit.message:
Manual override minigames: guide robots through physical tasks with light delay

Real Mars remote operations training. Robot is stuck → sends request →
you send commands one at a time → each command has light delay before
robot executes → correct sequence = fixed, wrong sequence = broken worse.

4 manual override scenarios:
- STUCK BOLT: perchlorate corrosion on ISRU fastener. Apply penetrant →
  wait → heat cycle → extract CCW. Force it or go clockwise = stripped.
- SOLAR PANEL CLEANING: dust removal with brush arm. Too much pressure
  scratches the anti-reflective coating permanently. Gentle sequence only.
- ANTENNA REALIGNMENT: comms dish pointing. Unlock → slew az → slew el →
  lock → verify. Skip the lock = dish vibrates in wind, no signal.
- AIRLOCK SEAL: O-ring replacement. Depressurize → remove → extract →
  seat → pressurize → verify. Pressurize before seating = blowout.

Each has trap commands that look right but aren't. Light delay means you
can't react instantly — you commit to a command and wait to see if it
worked. That's what Mars remote ops actually feels like.

Replaced drama tasks (crew disputes, morale votes) with real engineering.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Colony Risk Index + ambient hazards + traceable post-mortem

CRI (Colony Risk Index) — a VIX for Mars:
- Computed every sol by LisPy VM from 10 colony state variables
- Range 0-100: LOW → MODERATE → ELEVATED → HIGH → CRITICAL
- Displayed in status bar and ground HUD (color-coded)
- Feeds into riskRoll() — higher CRI = higher chance of bad outcomes
- Every decision's secondary consequence is now risk-weighted

Ambient hazards (fire every sol, no decision caused them):
- Micrometeorites: 0.1% base, strikes solar/ISRU/greenhouse/habitat
- Regolith infiltration: dust gets into seals, slow efficiency loss
- Solar particle events: unpredictable radiation spikes (shelter helps)
- Solar conjunction: comms blackout every ~669 sols, truly alone
- Equipment fatigue: everything degrades permanently after Sol 100

Post-mortem now traces the causal chain:
- Decision chain: last 8 player decisions with approve/deny/timeout
- Unintended consequences: all secondary effects that fired
- Ambient hazards endured: count of Mars-caused events
- Crew status at death: individual HP, morale, radiation, status
- System degradation: solar/ISRU/greenhouse efficiency at failure
- What might have saved you: specific actionable analysis

All probabilities sourced from NASA mission data and Mars physics.
Risk accumulates through decisions. Mars doesn't forget.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Deep consequences: every decision cascades through the colony

Papers Please tasks now have real downstream effects:
- Deny O₂ reroute → crew morale drops (they know you're gambling their air)
- Deny medical care → ENTIRE crew demoralizes ('they don't care about us')
- Deny rest twice → WORK STOPPAGE — zero production (you pushed too hard)
- Ignore robot diagnostic → real chance of permanent robot loss
- Ride out dust storm → permanent solar panel damage + radiation exposure
- Construction diverts crew → colony output drops during build
- Supply manifest → wrong choice and you wait 760 sols for the next window
- Crew disputes → side with one, the other remembers
- Power rationing → lights go dark, flashlights come out
- Water crisis → sacrifice greenhouse or dehydrate the crew
- Role reassignment after death → cross-train or spread thin

New task types: water crisis, power rationing, crew conflict, role
reassignment. Morale tasks escalate if previously denied. Robot
diagnostics have real failure probability if ignored.

Every mundane stamp has big consequences. That's Mars.

commit.message:
test: add branch-coverage tests for G4OCCTMaterialMapReader (#342)

* coverage: exclude EH phantom branches from gcovr branch count

Add --exclude-throw-branches and --exclude-unreachable-branches to the
gcovr invocation in cmake/Coverage.cmake.

Branch coverage reports a misleadingly low 53.8% because GCC generates
implicit "exception thrown" branch edges for every OCCT/Xerces/Geant4
callsite.  These branches appear as "Branch N not taken" even for
functions that never throw in practice.

--exclude-throw-branches removes these EH phantom edges from the count.
--exclude-unreachable-branches removes edges leading to lines with no
executable code (dead code identified in issues #329-#331).

Expected: branch coverage jumps from ~53.8% to ~70%+ with no new tests.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add branch-coverage tests for G4OCCTSolid

Add tests that exercise previously uncovered branches in G4OCCTSolid.cc:
- ComputeBounds: planar face with inner wires (wireCount>1 path), triggered
  by constructing a box-with-cylindrical-hole via BRepAlgoAPI_Cut
- TryInsertSphere: dominance check — a sphere at the box centre dominates
  nearby inscribed spheres (gap == |Dc|, condition fires at equality)
- TryInsertSphere: eviction — a flat box supplies 50+ non-dominated r~10mm
  spheres, pushing the cache from 15 initial entries to 65, triggering
  pop_back; a subsequent near-corner call (r~1mm) triggers the capacity
  early-exit when back().radius~2.5mm > 1mm
- DistanceToOut: non-planar solids (sphere, cylinder) exercise the
  fAllFacesPlanar=false BVH path instead of PlanarFaceLowerBoundDistance
- DistanceToIn: a diagonal exterior point inside the sphere AABB exercises
  the Tier-1 BVH lower-bound path (aabbDist=0, bvhDist>tol)
